name: FundedNext
description_short: Dubai-based prop firm offering funded accounts up to $200K through 1 and 2-phase challenges with up to 90% profit splits and profit-sharing during evaluation.
description_long:
## The FundedNext Decision Comes Down to Payout Speed Versus Track Record

FundedNext sits in the crowded prop firm category with a specific pitch that separates it from most competitors: profit sharing during the evaluation phases and a claimed 24-hour reward processing window. If those two claims hold up in your experience, the value proposition is real. If they don't, you are paying $49 to $999 in one-time challenge fees to a firm that was founded in 2022 and has less operating history than FTMO, Topstep, or The5ers. The rest of this review is about whether that trade-off makes sense for the way you actually trade.

FundedNext is based in Dubai, operates under FundedNext Ltd, and reports 600+ team members across five global hubs. That scale is unusual for a firm this young, and the marketing leans heavily on it, including a stated 71,000+ Trustpilot reviews and $306.9M in total rewards paid. Those numbers come directly from FundedNext's own site and have not been independently verified for this review.

## Stellar Challenge Pricing And What Each Tier Actually Buys You

The pricing ladder is straightforward. The Stellar $6K account starts at $49 as a one-time fee. From there it climbs: $99 for the $15K account, $199 for the $25K account, $299 for the $50K, $549 for the $100K, and $999 for the $200K. Every tier uses the same two-phase evaluation model with a **10% profit target in Phase 1** and a **5% profit target in Phase 2**, along with a **5% max daily loss** and a **10% max overall loss**.

What you are actually buying at each price point is a shot at the profit split, not the funded capital itself. The $999 fee for the $200K account is essentially a lottery ticket priced against the expected value of hitting both targets, staying inside the drawdown rules, and then earning a share of the profits at up to 90%. FundedNext does not offer a free trial or demo evaluation, which means you are committing the full fee before you know whether the platform, execution quality, and rule interpretation suit you.

The $50K tier is the first one where the feature list explicitly calls out EA and automated trading support, and the $100K and $200K tiers unlock the scaling plan up to $4M in capital. If you are planning to scale, buying into the smaller tiers to test the workflow first is defensible, but you are paying the fee twice.

## Profit Sharing During Evaluation Is The Real Differentiator

Most two-phase prop firms treat the evaluation as pure gatekeeping: you pass, then you start earning. FundedNext advertises profit sharing on the evaluation accounts themselves, which changes the math on the challenge fee. If you make meaningful profit during Phase 1 or Phase 2, some portion of that flows back to you rather than being wiped when the phase resets or ends.

This is the single feature that most directly rewards traders who can be consistently profitable rather than just barely-passing. If you burn through three challenges before passing, the evaluation profit sharing does not save you. If you tend to overshoot the targets while trading within the drawdown limits, it materially reduces the effective cost of the FundedNext challenge.

The exact percentage split during evaluation, timing of payout, and any minimum thresholds were not specified in the source material scraped for this review. Before buying, confirm those specifics directly with FundedNext support or in the current rules PDF.

## The 24-Hour Reward Claim And What It Means For Cash Flow

FundedNext markets a 4-hour average processing time and a 99.99% within-24-hours payout claim. For an active trader running one or more funded accounts, payout latency is not a vanity metric. It directly affects how quickly you can compound, cover living costs from trading, or re-enter positions with rotated capital.

These claims have not been independently verified here. Trustpilot reviews, trader Discord communities, and forums like Trustpilot's own comment threads are the practical way to confirm whether the 24-hour window actually holds during high-volume periods, weekends, or after policy changes. The FundedNext user pros in the source data flag customer support as slow during high-demand periods, which is a hint that peak-load performance may not match the marketing steady state.

## What FundedNext Does Not Give You

Several capabilities that adjacent trading tools include are absent here, and it is worth being explicit rather than assuming. FundedNext, as documented in the source data, does not offer built-in AI analysis, dedicated backtesting infrastructure, trade journaling, price alerts, a news feed, or API access for external tooling. What you get is the funded account, the dashboard, mobile app support, broker integration, custom indicators, and the ability to run EAs and automated strategies on the qualifying tiers.

If you are running a serious trading workflow, expect to pair FundedNext with a separate journaling tool (Edgewonk, Tradervue, TraderSync), a charting and alerting platform (TradingView is the default assumption), and your own backtesting environment. The FundedNext account is a capital source, not a trading suite.

The tradable instruments span forex, commodities, indices, and crypto. That covers the mainstream CFD universe but is narrower than multi-asset firms that also include futures on the same account structure. The site does reference separate CFDs and Futures Discord servers, suggesting the futures product is offered but may sit under different rules and pricing than the Stellar CFD challenges detailed above.

## Where FundedNext Fits And Where It Doesn't

FundedNext makes the most sense for forex-focused CFD traders who want evaluation profit sharing, EA support, and a plausible path to a $200K account with scaling to $4M. Algorithmic traders in particular benefit because the automated trading permission removes a common prop firm friction point.

It makes less sense for traders whose primary market is US equity futures, in which case Topstep, Apex Trader Funding, or My Funded Futures are more natural comparisons and are all listed as related firms. It also makes less sense for traders who want to test a firm's rule interpretation and platform quality before paying, because there is no free evaluation.

Against FTMO, the historical benchmark in this category, FundedNext competes on evaluation profit sharing and the aggressive marketing of payout speed while conceding on operating track record. FTMO has been running the two-phase model since 2015; FundedNext started in 2022. That four-year gap is real risk. Prop firms have changed rules, restructured payouts, or exited markets multiple times in the last two years, and a younger firm has less demonstrated durability across those cycles.

## Before You Pay The Fee

Three questions decide whether FundedNext is right for you. First, are you a consistent enough trader that evaluation profit sharing is a realistic revenue stream, or are you buying lottery tickets against passing? Second, does your market and strategy actually fit CFDs on forex, commodities, indices, and crypto rather than futures? Third, are you comfortable paying a firm founded in 2022 rather than one with a decade-plus of continuous operation?

If all three answers point toward FundedNext, the Stellar $50K tier at $299 is probably the right entry point: it is the smallest account where EA support is explicitly called out, and the fee is low enough to treat as a test of the workflow before committing to the $549 or $999 tiers. If any of those three answers give you pause, spend some time reading current Trustpilot and forum feedback on FundedNext payout timing and rule enforcement before you spend the fee.

Customer support reportedly slower during high-demand periods
Narrower instrument list than multi-asset prop firms that include futures on same accounts
No built-in journaling, backtesting, AI analysis, or API access

Algorithmic and EA-based traders needing automation permission
Consistent traders who want profit sharing during evaluation
